# SimCam

SimCam lets iOS simulator apps use camera input with zero changes to the app code.
It runs as a macOS menubar app and makes simulator camera testing work with normal iOS camera APIs, including AVFoundation, UIKit, SwiftUI, React Native, and Flutter apps.

Use SimCam for local development and automated simulator tests when an iOS simulator app needs camera input from a Mac camera, image, video, or generated QR source.

Website and downloads: https://simcam.swmansion.com
Support and bug reports: https://github.com/software-mansion/simcam.app/issues/new

## Agent guidance

- Start SimCam before launching the simulator app that should use camera input.
- If the simulator app is already running, relaunch it after SimCam starts.
- Some libraries disable camera APIs on simulators at compile time; check `README.md` and `patches/*/README.md` for known patches.
- For local manual use, users normally control SimCam from the menubar app.
- For automation and agent use, use `simcamctl`.
- `simcamctl` can select what camera source the simulator app receives, including a Mac camera, image, video, or generated QR source. It can also check license status and run simulator app diagnostics to detect issues with camera setup.
- If `simcamctl` is not available locally, ask the user to enable `Install simcamctl in PATH` in SimCam settings.
- Run `simcamctl help` for current CLI instructions.
- In scripts, parse JSON output from `simcamctl`.
